articles.title: Temporal: The 9-Year Journey to Fix Time in JavaScript
articles.description: JavaScript’s date/time handling is notoriously messy and libraries like Moment.js became popular as a way to work around it. In 2017, Maggie Johnson-Pint, a maintainer of Moment.js, proposed the Temporal API to fix date/time handling for good, and we’re mostly there (support is growing, with Safari and Node to catch up).
articles.author: Jason Williams (Bloomberg)

articles.title: Vite 8.0 Released
articles.description: A mega release for the popular build tool. Designed to be a smooth upgrade, there’s a lot behind the scenes: @vitejs/plugin-react v6 no longer needs Babel, Rolldown replaces Rollup and esbuild, Wasm SSR support, browser console forwarding to the terminal, and big performance gains.
articles.author: Vite

articles.title: Source Maps: Shipping Features Through Standards
articles.description: Source maps are JSON files that provide debuggers and similar tools with a mapping between minified/transformed code and the original codebase. Jon gives us a tour and takes us behind the scenes of how the feature has progressed towards becoming a standard (ECMA-426).
articles.author: Jon Kuperman (Bloomberg)

articles.title: How we Rewrote 130K Lines from React to Svelte in Two Weeks
articles.description: A common adage in recent months has been that the use of LLMs and coding agents could lock us into using only the most popular frameworks, but in reality they also make switching between frameworks easier than ever before.
articles.author: Strawberry

articles.title: Best Practices for Svelte Developers
articles.description: A brand new page in the Svelte docs that outlines some best practices for writing more robust Svelte apps.
articles.author: Svelte Docs

articles.title: An Empirical Study of Frontend Memory Leaks
articles.description: Analysis of five hundred React, Vue and Angular apps for patterns that lead to memory leaks. Missing timer cleanups and event listener removals cause the majority of problems.
articles.author: Ko-Hsin Liang

articles.title: Rewriting a 12-Year-Old JavaScript Library in TypeScript
articles.description: Specifically, the Machina finite state machine library.
articles.author: Jim Cowart

articles.title: Nuxt 4.4: The Full-Stack Vue Framework
articles.description: The full-stack Vue framework that includes routing (now powered by Vue Router v5), server-side rendering, and data fetching out of the box now adds custom useFetch/useAsyncData factories, typed layout props, build profiling, and more.
articles.author: Daniel Roe and the Nuxt Team

articles.title: Reveal.js 6.0: The HTML Presentation Framework
articles.description: A long-standing way to bring elegant presentations to anyone with a browser. v6.0 has some breaking changes, switches to Vite, and introduces an official React wrapper.
articles.author: Hakim El Hattab

articles.title: RedwoodSDK 1.0 Released: The Cloudflare-Native React Framework
articles.description: A server-first React framework, built as a Vite plugin, that integrates deeply with the Cloudflare platform (why?) and its provision of workers, databases (D1), durable objects, storage (R2), AI APIs, etc.
articles.author: Peter Pistorius

articles.description: Sarah Gooding rounds up last week's TC39 meeting and the proposals that advanced there, including Temporal, Import Text, Error Stack Accessor, and Iterator Includes.
😆 BigInt.PI = 3? Enjoy qntm's tongue-in-cheek proposal to add mathematical constants to BigInt.
Laurent Cazanove rounds up what was announced at last week's Vue.js Amsterdam conference, including Vue Router 5, Vite 8, and Void Cloud.
Electron 41.0 – The cross-platform desktop app framework adds ASAR Integrity digest and MSIX auto-updating support, improves Wayland support, and updates to Chromium 146, Node v24.14.0, and V8 14.6.
Nitro v3 Beta – Extend your Vite app with a production-ready server, compatible with any runtime. Handy if you want to try building your own framework!
Vitest 4.1 – Next-gen testing framework, now supporting Vite 8.
Preact 10.29.0, Prisma 7.5.0, Babel 8.0 RC3, Vue 3.6.0 Beta 8

articles.title: JPEG Compression
articles.description: A highly technical breakdown that looks at the underlying mechanics of JPEG compression and file size reduction and how the techniques behind it all lean on the bias of human vision and perception.
articles.author: Sophie Wang

articles.title: 📈 The 49MB Web Page
articles.description: Would you be surprised if loading a news article led to 422 network requests and 49MB of data transfer? This post reflects on the problems that have led to this being a common experience on news sites.
articles.author: Shubham Bose

articles.title: Monitor and Improve Your Web App’s Load Performance
articles.description: A new experimental ‘Network Efficiency Guardrails’ feature is available in Edge 146 that helps identify which resources, whether first or third-party, require optimization to improve load time. Feedback is being sought.
articles.author: Flores, Huang, Brosset (Microsoft Edge)

articles.title: contrast-color() Beyond Black and White
articles.description: This up and coming function is limited to returning either black or white right now, but Una shows us how to work around this and have it return a much wider array of colors.
articles.author: Una Kravets

articles.title: Abusing Customizable Selects
articles.description: A superb look at just how we can now transform standard HTML dropdowns thanks to the customizable select element. Fun demos aplenty here, including the above playing card variant.
articles.author: Patrick Brosset

articles.title: There is No “Wrong” in CSS
articles.description: As CSS allows us to solve a myriad of problems in a myriad of different ways, Jens makes the case for why we can’t really write ‘wrong’ CSS. Four reasons are listed. Do you agree?
articles.author: Jens Oliver Meiert

articles.title: An Empirical Study of Frontend Memory Leaks
articles.description: After running benchmarks and subsequent analysis of some five hundred repos, here’s a thorough look at the common patterns that often lead to memory leaks (broken down by framework).
articles.author: Ko-Hsin Liang

articles.title: Building Dynamic Toggletips with Anchored Container Queries
articles.description: With Chrome 143 we can now use anchored container queries, and Daniel takes a look at a potential future use case in creating 'toggletips'. A solid tutorial in which you’ll learn about popovers, anchor positioning, and what they can unlock.
articles.author: Daniel Schwarz

articles.title: Building a Scroll-Reactive 3D Gallery with Three.js, Velocity, and Mood-Based Backgrounds
articles.description: A look at how to create a neat scroll-driven WebGL gallery in Three.js (demo here) with depth-layered images, palette-driven backgrounds, and motion that responds to scroll velocity.
articles.author: Houmahani Kane

articles.title: Vite 8.0 Released
articles.description: A notable release for the popular frontend build tool, with significant performance and compatibility gains. This version adds Wasm SSR support, browser console forwarding to the terminal, and new integrated Devtools.
articles.author: Vite

articles.title: Astro 6.0 Released
articles.description: Introduces a built-in Fonts API, the Content Security Policy API, support for Live Content Collections, and more.
articles.author: Phillips, Stoppa, Lefebvre, Kane

articles.title: 📺 ArtPlayer: A Modern, Full-Featured HTML5 Video Player
articles.description: There's a live demo here. It gets you quite close to a YouTube-like experience.
articles.author: Harvey Zhao

articles.title: Starwind UI: A Component Library for Astro, Styled with Tailwind
articles.description: The author describes this as “shadcn/ui for Astro”, in reference to its seamless CLI integration. Includes 45+ free components covering a wide range of UI possibilities. Repo is here, and you can see the components in action in this theme builder.
articles.author: Branden

articles.title: The Color API: Color Conversion, Naming, Scheming & Placeholders
articles.description: Described as a “Swiss army knife for color”. Try out the API using the site or use the API programmatically to convert between formats, generate color schemes, and create placeholder images on the fly.
articles.author: Josh Beckman

articles.title: What's My 'Just Noticeable Difference'?
articles.description: Get ready to squint. This site tests your ability to spot subtle differences in color. It starts simple enough, but over the course of 40 rounds gets increasingly harder. Your success isn’t all down to your eyesight though, as what monitor you’re using plays a big part. There’s a related write up here that goes into more detail.
articles.author: Keith Cirkel
